channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump campaign, Musk coordinated to stop hacked material on X: Report
item.description: Former President Trump’s campaign worked with Elon Musk’s X to prevent links to hacked campaign materials from circulating on the social media platform last month, according to The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The IRS is rolling out its free tax-filing tool to 30 million Americans—and surprisingly, it’s great
item.description: Some 30 million Americans across the country will have a new way to file their taxes next spring: Direct File, a tool developed by the Internal Revenue Service that allows users to prepare their taxes and file online completely free of charge and without the need for any third-party provider.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Covert, Fast Company

item.title: After Hurricane Milton, a growing risk: Flooded electric cars going up in flames
item.description: Not every EV flooded by storm surge goes up in flames but it’s become frequent enough that insurers, car makers, fire chiefs and politicians have all issued warnings to EV owners.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Tesla’s Cybercab, Robovan revealed with Elon Musk’s reputation at stake
item.description: The product launch in California didn't address how Tesla will make the leap from selling advanced driver-assistance features to fully autonomous vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: What's behind the northern lights that dazzled the sky farther south than normal
item.description: The latest solar storm has triggered northern lights farther south than normal including into parts of Germany, the United Kingdom, New England and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Climate change gave significant boost to Milton's destructive rain, winds, scientists say
item.description: Scientists say human-caused climate change intensified deadly Hurricane Milton’s rainfall by 20 to 30% and strengthened its winds by about 10%.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Las entidades locales tendrán un espacio de datos para ordenar el tráfico, reducir la tarifa energética y planificar mejor la gestión urbana
item.description: “Un paso decisivo e innovador hacia la digitalización y modernización de nuestras ciudades que contribuya a mejorar los servicios públicos que se prestan a la ciudadanía”.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Senators press Commerce Department to boost transparency about CHIPS Act contracts
item.description: A group of senators are urging the Commerce Department to be more transparent regarding the terms of agreements made under the CHIPS and Science Act, arguing the agency must ensure the U.S. semiconductor industry is “safe and sustainable.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ben Horowitz’s dual support for Trump and Harris says a lot about the Valley’s AI politics
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Marriott settles with the FTC for $52 million over data breaches
item.description: Marriott International has agreed to pay $52 million and make changes to bolster its data security to resolve state and federal claims related to major data breaches that affected more than 300 million of its customers worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Faster Acquisition Pathway Considered for Artificial Intelligence - A Game-Changer for Defense Department?
item.description: The US Defense Department explores the possibility of creating a separate acquisition pathway or sub-path specifically designed for artificial intelligence, aiming to accelerate its development and delivery.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft unveils health care AI tools
item.description: Microsoft unveiled several new artificial intelligence (AI) tools on Thursday aiming to support health care organizations through medical imaging models, health care agent services and an AI-driven workflow solution for nurses.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, The Hill

item.title: How a Cold War plan to stop nuclear proliferation could protect the world from an AI arms race
item.description: As Mark Twain said, “History doesn’t often repeat itself, but it does rhyme.” We are fortunate that it does, because it means we can use lessons from the past at least as a rough guide to the present.
item.dc:creator: Ash Jogalekar, Fast Company

item.title: Trials for 2019 kidnapping, killing of Santa Cruz tech entrepreneur divided into four
item.description: Attorneys have argued in court filings that a fair trial was not possible because several of the defendants implicated each other during post-arrest interviews with police detectives, and that transcript redactions would be difficult to untangle for each.
item.dc:creator: Jessica York, The Mercury News

item.title: Scammers exploit grief with fake funeral streaming on Facebook
item.description: Scammers are offering video streaming for funeral services and taking advantage of people who are grieving to get credit card information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FinOps SaaS: The missing piece of the puzzle
item.description: With more and more businesses moving to the Cloud, FinOps is becoming a vital framework for efficiently controlling Cloud expenses.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Say goodbye to dead batteries with this portable wind turbine
item.description: The Shine 2.0 is a 3-in-1 portable wind turbine, charge controller and power bank that fits into a backpack and works day and night, rain or shine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon’s new AI-powered spotlight tech aims to help delivery drivers find packages faster
item.description: Amazon, in its quest for greater efficiency, has developed new systems to shave seconds off each package delivery and to help customers make faster buying choices, even for new product types that they may know little about.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Twin boys born conjoined celebrate 1st birthday after separation surgery
item.description: Twin brothers who were born conjoined recently celebrated their first birthday after undergoing successful separation surgery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Volunteers at Concordia Cemetery use technology to unearth Buffalo history
item.description: "We're going to be looking for and bringing up some of the tombstones that have sunk or have been buried," said Bonnie Fleischauer, Concordia Cemetery's public information officer.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders: Mark Cuban wrong to call for Lina Khan to be replaced
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) publicly disagreed with Mark Cuban after he called for Federal Trade Commissioner (FTC) Chair Lina Khan to be replaced if Vice President Harris is elected president. In a post on the social platform X, Sanders said Cuban “is wrong” when he called for a new FTC chair.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles
item.description: The government needs to push the market in the direction of secure-by-design, including manufacturing, fielding and management of these new endpoints. The post IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Red Sea cable cuts disrupted up to 70% of traffic, says RETN
item.description: Independent global network services provider RETN says its network diagnostics show that the February 2024 cable cuts in the Red Sea, initially reported to have affected 25% of data traffic between Europe and Asia, caused far more disruption than originally estimated.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Embracing your inner on-premises self
item.description: In a significant development in the cloud computing industry, AWS, a leading hyperscaler, has recently acknowledged considerable competition from on-premises IT solutions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: California’s vetoed AI bill: Bullet dodged, but not for long
item.description: Artificial intelligence has the power to revolutionize industries, drive economic growth, and improve our quality of life. But like any powerful, widely available technology, AI also poses significant risks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SAP Build gains AI capabilities to help build autonomous agents
item.description: SAP wants developers to view its Build platform as the one extension solution for all of SAP’s applications, according to Michael Aneling, chief product officer for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
item.dc:creator: Info World
